name: Stockholm Metro Art Gallery
description: Stockholm's decidedly unique public transit system has doubled as the world's longest art gallery for decades.

name: Codex Gigas (The Devil's Bible)
description: An illuminated manuscript comprising the life's work of a lone monk, inexplicably decorated with a portrait of Satan himself.

name: R1 Nuclear Reactor
description: Sweden's first nuclear reactor, R1, can be visited 25 meters underground.

name: Aifur
description: Dine on food tied to Viking history and legend at this subterranean Stockholm spot.

name: Victoriahuset (Victoria House)
description: A rare surviving Victorian greenhouse designed specifically to house giant water lilies.

name: Vasa Museum
description: It houses the remains of a 17th-century version of the Titanic.

name: Kungsträdgården Metro Station
description: Stockholm's deepest subway station is also a stunning ecological wonder.

name: Stadsbiblioteket (Stockholm Public Library)
description: The library's graceful rotunda is like a bibliophile's temple.

name: Colour by Numbers
description: This Stockholm light tower can be controlled by anyone with a smartphone.

name: Birthplace of the Stockholm Syndrome
description: Now a hotel, this former bank was the site of a robbery that spawned a psychological phenomenon.

name: Sweden Solar System
description: The world's largest model of our planetary system.

name: Skansen Open Air Museum
description: Sweden's "good old days" painstakingly re-created in a model village.

name: Uppland Runic Inscription 53
description: An ancient Viking runestone hidden in plain view in Stockholm's old town.

name: Ribbinska Huset
description: It is said that each of the white stones in the red facade of this medieval house represents the head of a murdered Swedish noble.

name: Snösätra Wall of Fame
description: Street artists transformed this abandoned industrial neighborhood into one of Europe's largest graffiti exhibitions.

name: Treehotel
description: A series of modernist treehouse hotel rooms grace the outskirts of an Arctic forest.

name: Mårten Trotzigs Gränd
description: Stockholm's narrowest alley is barely over two feet wide.

name: Museum of Medieval Stockholm
description: The discovery of an old wall led to the creation of this fascinating museum about the city in the Middle Ages.

name: Pionen Data Center
description: The internet as provided by a James Bond villain.

name: Trollskogen (The Troll Forest)
description: Crooked windswept pines give this old forest an enchanted and magical look.

name: Icehotel
description: The world's first ice hotel is still one of the most elaborate and beautiful.

name: Nobels spränggropar (Nobel's Blasting Bunkers)
description: The remains of Alfred Nobel's first nitroglycerine factory can still be found on the shores of a Swedish bay.

name: Solar Egg
description: A golden egg-shaped sauna in the Arctic tundra.

name: 'Hemlös räv' ('Rag and Bone')
description: This homeless brass fox tugs at your heartstrings from the foot of a Stockholm bridge.

name: Stadshuskällaren
description: Eat a Nobel banquet from any year in the basement of Stockholm's City Hall.

name: 'The Battle of Gotland 1361' Exhibit
description: This grisly exhibit houses skeletons and weapons from a devastating 14th-century clash.

name: The Cuckold of Skeppsbron
description: A man has revenged his true love's betrayal through tears and stone genitalia.

name: Disgusting Food Museum
description: This collection of 80 polarizing foods aims to challenge your subjective notions of repulsion.

name: Hey STHLM
description: A Japanese video game arcade hides in the back of a Swedish pool hall.

name: STF Kolarbyn Eco Lodges
description: Bring your hiking shoes, wool socks, and thermal underwear for a stay in these cozy forest huts.

name: Omnipollo's Church
description: Located in an old church, this brewery produces a different kind of holy water.

name: Älgens Hus (The Moose House)
description: A Swedish farm is the world's only producer of the rare, creamy delicacy that is moose cheese.

name: Järnpojke
description: Stockholm's tiniest statue is of a small boy looking at the Moon.

name: Nimis in Ladonia
description: Micro-nation based around a nine-story high illegally built tower.

name: Malm Whale
description: One of the world's few mounted blue whales, its jaws are hinged open allowing visitors to descend into a lounge.

name: Ales Stenar
description: According to legend, this boat-shaped henge is built over the grave of a mythic king.

name: Stockholm Observatory
description: When this 18th-century observatory's meridian was moved, it set Sweden's time back by 0.04 seconds.

name: Last Home of Descartes
description: A plaque marks the house in Stockholm where the French philosopher René Descartes lived up until his death.

name: Øresund Bridge
description: This chimerical bridge/tunnel appears to dead end right into the sea.

name: Lost Toys of the Nybohov Funicular
description: No one knows who's responsible for decorating this tunnel with stuffed animals, but the people love it enough to protest their removal.

name: Kymlinge Metro Station
description: "Only the dead get off at Kymlinge."

name: Old Tjikko
description: The world's oldest individual clonal tree looks like a wimp despite standing tall for 9,550 years.

name: Ytterby Mine
description: The single richest source of elemental discoveries in the world.

name: Newton's Apple
description: This giant subway sculpture celebrates the fruit that inspired the theory of gravity.

name: Västra Skogen Escalator
description: A Stockholm metro station is home to an escalator that is not only the longest in Sweden, but among the longest in the world.

name: Borrås Skåra
description: Walking through this narrow gorge will make you feel like you've fallen into a fairytale landscape.

name: Wooden Horse Museum
description: This small museum nestled inside a souvenir shop is dedicated to Sweden's most well-known toy.

name: Neptuni Åkrar
description: An otherworldly, fossil-rich cobble beach holds Viking graves, cairns, and other remains.

name: Utter Inn
description: A floating underwater hotel, created by a Swedish artist.

name: Östermalm Echo Dome
description: Sound travels strangely in this underground whispering gallery.

name: Rök Rune Stone
description: This encrypted monolith protected a secret of Norse mythology by hiding in a church wall for over a millennium.

name: Björn Järnsidas Hög (Björn Ironsides Mound)
description: The suspected burial mound of legendary Viking Björn Ironside.

name: Jumbo Stay
description: Frequent fliers welcome in this airplane hotel.

name: Vikingaliv Runestone
description: Outside a Viking museum sits a modern-day runestone made by a Swedish runemaster using traditional methods.

name: The Royal Armoury
description: A king's bloodstained shirt is among the gruesome collection of artifacts displayed below Stockholm's Royal Palace.

name: Ragnhildsholmen Castle Ruin
description: This castle ruin in the middle of nowhere is a gem for wildlife enthusiasts.

name: Astrid Lindgrens Hem (Astrid Lindgren's House)
description: The apartment where Pippi Longstocking came to life.

name: Kyrkö Car Cemetery
description: A peat bog in southern Sweden provides a cozy and photogenic home for junked Volvos, Saabs, and the occasional American muscle car.

name: Gustavianum Museum
description: The second oldest anatomical theater is housed in a museum of curiosities.

name: Gotland
description: Largest island in the Baltic Sea experiences such idyllic weather that roses bloom in the middle of December.

name: Borgvattnet Vicarage
description: Said to be one of Sweden's most haunted places, this vicarage once even attracted a ghost-busting priest.

name: Säter Museum of Mental Health
description: A museum dedicated to the history of psychiatric treatment featuring a gallery of art made by patients.

name: Love Locks of Västerbron
description: A former suicide bridge is now accumulating symbols of love instead of a body count.

name: Katarina Elevator
description: This gem from the industrial age helps visitors and residents alike traverse hilly Stockholm.

name: Arlanda Airport Runestone
description: An 11th-century runestone decorates the halls of Terminal 2 of this Scandinavian travel hub.

name: Li Grave Stones
description: Iron Age grave stones and Viking burials, a short drive from Gothenburg.

name: Lappis Scream
description: Every Tuesday, students let off steam by screaming into the night air.

name: 'Humor'
description: This unique sculpture is often overlooked by pedestrians.

name: Gothenburg Medical History Museum
description: A 200-year-old collection of medical paraphernalia in a historic house.

name: Villa Villekulla
description: Visit the home of Sweden's beloved fictional character, Pippi Longstocking.

name: Royal Waiting Hall
description: Although rarely used, there is still a hidden room for the royal family at the Stockholm central station.

name: 'The Knotted Gun'
description: This poignant sculpture of a gnarled weapon is a symbol of non-violence.

name: Abandoned Eriksdal Train Tunnel
description: It was meant to help transport wounded soldiers to a secret underground hospital.

name: Sigtuna Stora Gatan
description: Sweden's oldest street has not changed for nearly a millennium.

name: Jättegrytorna i Tararp (The Tarap Giant Kettles)
description: A geological wonder that helped fuel myths of trolls and giants.

name: HSB Paternoster lift
description: Stockholm's last remaining paternoster elevator.

name: 'Två Myror' ('Two Ants')
description: Enormous ants wielding giant pine needles guard a Stockholm real estate company's office.

name: Stockholm Metro Ventilation Art
description: Small Easter eggs are hidden in the ventilation holes of the city's newest trains.

name: Skepptuna Church
description: A 12th-century church with a medieval fresco and an intriguing “Greek runestone.”

name: Källargränd urinoar (Källargränd Urinal)
description: A 130-year-old urinal is the oldest of Stockholm and perhaps all of Sweden.

name: Stomatol Sign
description: Sweden's oldest animated sign is still lighting up the skyline today.

name: Prästens Badkar (The Priests Bathtub)
description: The world's only above water sand volcano, believed to once be the bathtub of a priest.

name: Båstnäs Car Cemetery
description: This Swedish automobile graveyard is a beautiful gallery of decay, but woe be to any who would steal a souvenir.

name: Bexell's Talking Stones
description: Hundreds of mossy stones engraved with bits of philosophy, literature and random musings.

name: Battle of the Elms
description: The scars of a remarkably organized public protest to save 13 elm trees are still visible in this Stockholm park.

name: Gävlebocken
description: If there is one thing you don't want to be in Sweden during Christmas, it's a Yule Goat.

name: Alfred Nobel's Grave
description: The final resting place of the man whose name has become synonymous with greatness.

name: Bengt Erland Fogelberg's Norse God Statues
description: Finally reunited, the three effigies now stand in a museum courtyard.

name: Visingsö Oak Forest
description: A forest of immensely tall and unusually straight oak trees planted nearly 200 years ago to build naval ships that never came to be.

name: Laver Ruins
description: The concrete skeletons of an abandoned Swedish town hide among the trees.

name: Turning Torso
description: Swedish skyscraper with a 90° twist.

name: Rock Carvings in Tanum
description: Despite the groans of archaeologists, this astounding groups of Bronze Age petroglyphs have been painted red for tourists.

name: Arnold's Cannibal Museum
description: A former hen house turned curiosity cabinet displays one man's lifelong fascination with cannibalism.

name: Percys Trappa
description: A wooden street sign is the last remnant of this 400-year-old alleyway.

name: Sigurdsristningen (Sigurd Carving)
description: Stone carvings record the Norse myth of Sigurd, providing a glimpse into Scandinavia's transition from paganism to Christianity.

name: Kastellet Stockholm
description: The Swedish flag over this small naval fort signals that the nation is at peace.

name: Entombed Cross
description: This giant crucifix is like U2's Joshua Tree for Swedish death metal fans.

name: Olof Palme Memorial Plaque
description: A golden plaque marks the site of the mysterious unsolved assassination of the former Swedish prime minister.

name: Samkönade Trafikljusen
description: A temporary art piece to celebrate LGBTQ Pride is now a permanent part of Stockholm's traffic landscape.

name: Horologium mirabile Lundense
description: This Swedish astronomical clock was in storage for almost a century before its glorious workings were put on display once more.

name: Långholmen Crash Site
description: A steel paper plane is all that remains of a crashed aircraft that caused an amazingly small amount of damage.

name: Lummelunda Cave
description: This fantastic cave remained unexplored until 1948 when three schoolboys discovered a hidden entrance.

name: A Pile of Art
description: An eclectic sculpture park rises atop a giant pile of ashes in Sweden.

name: Trollvägen Magic Hill
description: Gravity seems to fail at this intriguing Swedish attraction.

name: Hallwylska Museet (Hallwyl Museum)
description: The 19th-century home of Count and Countess von Hallwyl is now a museum.

name: World of Volvo
description: The place to be if you love vintage Swedish cars.

name: On the Way
description: Enigmatic bug-like sculptures mystify drivers along a Swedish highway.

name: The First Grave of René Descartes
description: Inside this church, a monument honors the first burial site of the famed philosopher.

name: AlbaNova Telescope
description: Sweden's largest optical telescope sits on a rooftop in Stockholm.

name: Krutbanan (Gunpowder Track)
description: This railroad track was so secretive that it was omitted from all maps.

name: The Lion of Gripsholm Castle
description: This wonky bit of historical taxidermy is experiencing a second life online.

name: Gnisvärd Stone Ships
description: Ancient stone graves shaped like ships provided a Viking-like burial to carry the dead to the afterlife.

name: Stortorget Cannonball
description: Several cannonballs of mysterious origins decorate two buildings in Stortoget's main square.

name: Normalhöjdpunkten
description: Hidden in plain sight, Sweden's official ordnance datum is now abandoned.

name: Jakriborg
description: This cobblestoned medieval-style town was entirely constructed in the 1990s and 2000s.

name: Tumlehed Rock Paintings
description: Sweden's southernmost prehistoric rock art is hidden just outside Gothenburg.

name: Gryteskogen Troll Trees
description: This collection of Dwarf beech trees were once believed to be enchanted trolls in disguise.

name: Bistro Nobel Signed Chairs
description: Maybe the only restaurant in the world where your seat will be signed by a Nobel laureate.

name: Galgberget Gallows
description: This trio of stone spires claims to be the only preserved medieval execution site in Europe.

name: Kymlingestenen
description: A traveling Bronze Age sacrificial stone that was used to honor elves, fairies, and other woodland spirits.

name: Feskekôrka - The "Fish Church"
description: A Gothic-inspired fish market is Gothenburg's best place to find loaves and fishes.

name: Åtvidaberg Sun Cannon
description: In the town of Åtvidaberg, the midday sun sets off a loud cannon boom.

name: Royal Mounds of Gamla Uppsala
description: Ancient burial grounds of the wealthy.

name: Grand Circus Hotel
description: At this bohemian accommodation, guests who dream of joining the circus can spend the night in themed wagons.

name: Runestone in Gamla Uppsala Church
description: This mysterious runestone embedded in a church wall is linked to the medieval cult of Saint Erik.

name: Gamla Riksarkivet Lift
description: Sweden's first electric elevator remains hidden within a fortress-like building.

name: Carl XII's Oak
description: An ancient tree that was once the favorite of a young Swedish royal.

name: 'Svampen'
description: A concrete mushroom that divided Stockholm residents for nearly a century.

name: Bokbåten
description: Twice a year, this floating library delivers thousands of books to the remote islands of Stockholm’s archipelago.

name: The Flogsta Scream
description: A nightly tradition fills the streets of Flogsta with a collective scream.

name: Nipgubben
description: This tiny Swedish troll protects the land and all who wander within it.

name: MiniNobel
description: On Nobel Street, a miniature statue of Alfred Nobel.

name: Winter Lights
description: For a couple of months each year this Swedish city turns into a festive neon phantasmagoria.

name: Maritiman
description: The world's biggest floating museum in Gothenburg, Sweden.

name: Ängelholm UFO Memorial
description: A memorial to a Swedish hockey player's "encounter" with aliens.

name: 'In Between'
description: What looks like a birch tree smushed between two pieces of glass often confounds commuters.

name: Lycksele Ice Pillar
description: This small town's local tradition is to create the world's tallest ice pillar.

name: Tantolunden Flak Position
description: Tucked away in a downtown Stockholm park are the remains of a World War II defense position.

name: Sandby Borg Ringfort
description: Archaeologists are still unraveling the secrets of the brutal massacre that wiped out this ancient fort.

name: Pelle Svanslös Crosswalk
description:
Uppsala's adorable feline sign celebrates a popular children's book set in the Swedish
city.

name: Tjelvar's Grave
description: A Bronze Age stone ship said to be the grave of the mythical founder of Gotland.

name: Republic of Önneköp
description: This micronation's flag honors its favorite drink, Kaffegöken, or "cuckoo-coffee."

name: Old Police Station at Slottsskogen
description: The charming storybook building now houses a small police museum.

name: Dalahästen
description: The world's biggest version of the popular Swedish souvenir.

name: 'Thing of all Swedes' Mound
description: The official meeting point for the oddly named governing assembly of ancient Sweden.

name: Alvastra Abbey
description: The ruins of a monastery where Saint Bridget of Sweden had her revelations recorded.

name: Pyramid of Stjärneborg
description: This pyramid in southern Sweden houses the remains of an eccentric nobleman, who spent his life doing whatever he felt like doing.

name: Aunt Green, Aunt Brown, and Aunt Lavender
description: The three colorful women depict characters from a beloved children's story.

name: Njupeskärs Vattenfall
description: Believed to be the country's tallest waterfall, the Njupeskär is actually much smaller.

name: The Anti-Saxophone Sign at SSE
description: Absolutely no saxophones in the library.

name: Urdar's Well and Yggdrasil
description: Legend says this fabled well held part of a mythical Norse tree.

name: Little Istanbul
description: One man's fascination with Turkey led to this backyard mini-city.

name: Greta Garbo Statue of Integrity
description: This statue, deep in a Swedish forest, is all alone, just like the renowned recluse would have wanted.

name: Anundshög
description: This patch of land is home to Sweden's largest burial mound, allegedly belonging to a mythical king.

name: The Dead Falls of Lake Ragunda
description: A botched 18th-century canal silenced one of Sweden's most magnificent waterfalls.

name: Paltzerian
description: A tiny village is home to the world's only diner completely devoted to the many varieties of traditional Swedish dumplings.

name: Hven
description: This Scandinavian island is the site of the first modern observatory.

name: The Waterfall of Kvarnbyn Mölndal
description: Once the center of power for the region's various factories.

name: Dykande Måsen
description: A concrete Picasso in the middle of a student housing complex on the outskirts of Stockholm.

name: Stockholms Geographical Midpoint
description: The center of Stockholm can be found along this quiet street.

name: Drottningholms Slottsteater
description: 18th century opera in its original splendor.

name: Haga Slottsgrund
description: The palace for King Gustav III was never completed, but the foundation remains tucked away in this city park.

name: Pompes Grav (Pompe the Dog's Grave)
description: A tombstone on the grounds of the royal castle marks the burial site of a Swedish king's beloved dog.

name: Stockholm University Arboretum
description: A collection of trees from various corners of the world call Stockholm University home.

name: 'Morgondagens Väder' ('Tomorrow's Weather')
description: An abstract representation of weather in Sweden.

name: Äventyrsgruvan Tuna-Hästberg (Tuna-Hästberg Adventure Mine)
description: A centuries-old iron mine is now a paradise for explorers, divers, and spelunkers.

name: World's Largest Cheese Slicer
description: The cheese slicer may be a Norwegian invention, but the biggest ones in the world are found in Sweden.

name: ABBA the Museum
description: There's something in the air as history repeats itself at this museum dedicated to the Swedish pop band.

name: Gs 13 Runestone
description: A Viking man's tribute to his brother stands in Gävle's oldest church.

name: The First Pirate Bay Server
description: From under the bed of one of the co-founders, this server helped launch the notorious file-sharing website.

name: 'The Flute Player'
description: This small statue hints at the incorrect origins of the lush landscape park surrounding it.

name: Järnblocket (Iron Boulder)
description: A prehistoric geological oddity tucked behind Stockholm's natural history museum.

name: Café i Fårhus
description: This restaurant gives a new meaning to farm-to-table cuisine.

name: Gislövshammar
description: These mysterious circular formations are actually remnants of 19th century gristmills.

name: Bålberget Memorial
description: This serene wooded hilltop has a dark history as the climactic point of the Swedish witch trials.

name: Pippi Longstocking's House
description: A replica of the beloved character's house, modeled after the author's own drawings.

name: Aeroseum
description: This declassified Cold War bunker hosts a large collection of Swedish aircraft.

name: Västerås Historiska Skeppsmuseum (Västerås Historical Ship Museum)
description: This museum of Viking vessels allows visitors to take a replica out for a spin.

name: The Maiden's Tower
description: Known locally as the Jungfrutornet, this Medieval tower hides a dark legend.

name: Labyrinten vid Tibble (The Tibble Labyrinth)
description: This 2,000-year-old stone maze is one of the oldest in the country, but its purpose is still a mystery.

name: Yangtorp Sanctuary
description: A Chinese meditation center in the middle of rural Sweden, partly funded by a member of ABBA.

name: Sankt Göransporten Arrow Slit
description: A catapult rock from Gotland’s 13th-century civil war is still lodged in Scandinavia's best-preserved medieval city wall.

name: Lenin Spa
description: Take a schvitz just like an infamous Soviet dictator while his unblinking eye looks on.

name: MidSweden 365
description: A former top secret military tunnel is now stuffed with snow and open year-round to skiers.

name: Uggleviksreservoaren
description: This poured-concrete water reservoir is a gem of modernist architecture well-hidden in a city forest.

name: The Grave of Morgan
description: A simple stone remembers one of the most menacing horses in the Swedish military.

name: Talking Trash Cans of Malmö
description: These waste baskets play a few risque messages to discourage littering.

name: Cedergrenska Tower
description: Despite dreams of constructing this fairytale mansion, Albert Gotthard Nestor Cedergren never saw its completion.

name: Artipelag
description: This Stockholm art museum integrates many of its pieces into the surrounding outdoor areas and natural landscape.

name: Newton's Apple Tree
description: A living descendant of the famous tree that helped Isaac Newton develop the theory of gravity.

name: 'Highway'
description: Locals rallied to save this beloved mural after someone mistakenly attempted to cover it.

name: Johanneshovs Skans (Johanneshov Sconce)
description: Part of an abandoned19th-century fortress hides in plain sight inside a modern office building.

name: Växjö Echo Tower
description: The dome-shaped reservoir of this old water tower produces unique acoustic effects.

name: Dalhalla
description: The unique design of this former quarry provides ideal acoustics for concerts.

name: Uppland Runic Inscription 489
description: A rare rune stone dedicated to a woman.

name: Lego Åhléns City
description: A miniature model of the iconic Stockholm department store.

name: Jättegrytan i Skarpnäck (Skarpnäck Giant's Kettle)
description: This hole formed by a glacier is the largest and best-preserved of its kind in Stockholm.

name: Lyells Ek (Lyell's Oak)
description: This tree was once used to calculate the decrease of the Baltic Sea.

name: Västerbottensost Visitors Center
description: This little gallery is devoted to one small town's passion for a very special cheese.

name: Esrange Rocket Shelters
description: The wilderness of northern Sweden is full of shelters to protect people from sounding rockets returning from space.

name: Shipwreck of Kastellholmen
description: At low tide, you can see the remains of a 17th-century ship just off a Stockholm island.

name: Jokkmokk Market
description: Hang with reindeer at this 400-year-old Sámi market.

name: Bergsala’s Super Mario
description: A statue of the heroic plumber stands outside a company that bluffed its way into being Nintendo’s Scandinavian distributor.

name: Margareta Krook's Statue
description: A heated statue pays tribute to one of Sweden's great actresses.

name: Storsjon's Highway Ice Bridge
description: Winter roadtrips are a little more exciting on this seasonal, slippery expansion of Sweden's national highway system.

name: Tjurkö Ship Cemetery
description: For decades, these ships have been lying in rust awaiting a final decision on their fate.

name: Den Optiska Telegrafen (The Optical Telegraph)
description: The fastest way to send a message before electricity was invented.

name: Universitetshuset Runestone Circle
description: A large collection of ancient runestones decorates this university courtyard.

name: Malmo Möllestenen (The Malmo Millstone)
description: This millstone commemorates a gruesome tale that may have been the source of the city's name.

name: Sankt Olof Holy Springs
description: These two springs are tied to several legends, one of which involves the travels of Saint Olof.

name: Figurines of Borås
description: These miniature works of art are hiding in plain sight on buildings around the city.

name: 1/4 Milestone of Stockholm
description: This stone obelisk is a relic of the 17th-century Swedish mile markers.

name: Springkällan (The Spring)
description: An oil drilling mistake created this fountain that has been spraying water for over 150 years.

name: High Chaparral Wild West Theme Park
description: The lost American era of gunfighters, bandits, Indians and steam trains is recreated in Sweden.

name: 'Moby dick'
description: A statue that stood at the center of a long copyright battle.

name: Bockaby Urban Warfare Facility
description: This abandoned urban warfare facility is a dream come true for airsoft and paintball enthusiasts.

name: Joseph Kraus's Grave
description: The composer known as the "Swedish Mozart" arranged to be buried in an unusual spot.

name: Rune Staff Collection
description: The second-largest collection of runic calendar staffs in Sweden.

name: Smygehuk Utkiksplats (Sweden's Southernmost Point)
description: Sweden's southernmost point is located in this charming fishing village.

name: Lovikkavanten (Lovikka Mitten Statue)
description: The world’s largest knitted mitten is displayed in one of Sweden’s smallest Arctic villages.

name: Kungstornen
description: This pair of unsuspecting buildings are in fact the very first modern skyscrapers in Europe.

name: LKAB’s Visitor Center
description: The world's largest underground iron ore mine.

name: King Chulalongkorn Memorial
description: An ornate Thai pavilion in the middle of Sweden.

name: Tomb of Anders Celsius
description: A small church in Uppsala hides the grave of a renowned 18th-century astronomer.

name: Berzelius Park
description: This statue and park honor the Swedish chemist Jacob Berzelius, one of the founders of modern chemistry.

name: Vega Monument
description: Celebrating the Swedish ship used in the first Arctic expedition to navigate through the Northeast Passage.

name: Limhamn Limestone Quarry
description: This quarry filled with 65 million-year-old limestone is now home to a nature reserve.

name: Nasoteket (The Nose Academy)
description: Home to 200 plaster casts of Scandinavian proboscises.

name: Lego Swedish 1-M Solar Telescope
description: A 1:42 scale model of the world's second largest lensed telescope.

name: Giant Wooden Bear of Sveg
description: This massive Trojan ursine was built to be a new tourist symbol for a small Swedish town.

name: Shopping
description: The world's first purpose-built indoor shopping mall opened in Sweden in 1955.

name: Dragon Gate
description: Not quite the cultural gateway between China and Sweden its designer imagined.

name: Forsby-Köping Limestone Cableway
description: The remnants of a record-setting industrial bucket system can still be found dotting the Swedish countryside.

name: Lövestad Carved Statues
description: Rocks carved with biblical passages in the 1800s.

name: Djurgården Anti-Tank Barrier
description: Not far from the busy city lie these remains of a time when the world was on fire.

name: Tyska Kyrkan Weather Vane
description: This centuries-old rooster survived a devastating fire.

name: Haga Tingshus Jättegryta (Haga Courthouse Giant's Kettle)
description: This building, previously used as a courthouse, has an unusual geological feature in its basement.

name: Falu Gruva (Falun Mine)
description: A centuries-old copper mine that once provided two thirds of the world's copper supply.

name: Intighet (Nothingness)
description: The indecision over what to place in this square was immortalized with a plaque.

name: Fällbro Runestone (Uppland Runic Inscription Fv1946;258)
description: This Viking Age memorial contains the oldest known image of a Täby resident.

name: Luleå Ice Roads
description: Explore the frozen river and take a walk to a nearby island on some of the longest ice roads in Sweden.

name: Marathon March
description: A competition inspired by Stephen King's psychological horror novel "The Long Walk."

name: Stockholm Metro Red Line Seats
description: There are hidden patterns on the subway seats of some Stockholm metro cars.

name: Silverpilen (Silver Arrow)
description: Stockholm's famed ghost train is now on display at an art park in northern Sweden.

name: Simrislundsristningen (Simrislund Carvings)
description: These Bronze Age rock carvings are among the oldest in the region.

name: Bling-Bling for the People
description: Crystal chandeliers brighten up this dark underpass.

name: Stockholm Meteorological Station
description: One of the world's oldest continuously operating meteorological stations sits on a hill in Stockholm.

name: Saint Erik's Spring
description: Legend has it this spring formed at the spot where a medieval Swedish king was killed.

name: Amalia Eriksson Statue
description: The trailblazing Swedish businesswoman created a beloved minty candy called polkagris.

name: Gamla Riksbanken (The Old National Bank)
description: One of the world's first dedicated bank buildings in the world is now the oldest one.

name: Scharmer's Small Solar Telescope
description: A scale model of the world's largest existing lensed telescope.

name: Trojaborg
description: Legend has it that this large rock maze in Sweden was created as punishment by the daughter of a wealthy captain.

name: Fet-Mats grav (Grave of Fet-Mats)
description: His body was found mysteriously preserved in a Swedish mine over 40 years after his initial disappearance.

name: Runstenskullen (The Rune Stone Hill)
description: These ancient rune stones plucked from the countryside now call this university park home.

name: Sö 90 Runestone
description: This runestone once destroyed by an explosion has been painstakingly reassembled to save its history.

name: Skulptur i Pilane
description: This Swedish sculpture park is a unique combination of ancient ruins and modern art.

name: Walking to Boras
description: A colossal strolling Pinocchio that was greeted with more outrage than whimsy.

name: "Oh Yeah Woo Yeah"
description: The rock carving that became an internet meme.

name: Gålö Seal Training Station
description: This secret facility trained seals for the Swedish navy during the Second World War.

name: Täby kyrka (Täby  Church)
description: A medieval church that contains murals from famed artist Albertus Pictor.

name: Mörksugga Statue
description: A cuddly version of a dark mythological creature now acts as the mascot of a town.

name: Anders Celsius's Thermometer
description: The first thermometer made with the almost-universal temperature scale is backwards.

name: Gantofta sliprännor
description: Hidden in an enchanting ravine, numerous mysterious sandstone grooves hint at ancient pagan rituals.

name: Gagnef Flottbron
description: This unique bridge is one of the last of its kind in Sweden.

name: Sölvesborgsbron (The Sölvesborg Bridge)
description: One of the longest footbridges in Europe.

name: Wilhelmina Ice Pole Sitting Contest
description: Locals perched on icy thrones brave frigid temperatures during this bizarre winter tradition.

name: World's Smallest Dala Horse
description: This Dalecarlian horse is about the size of a pinhead.

name: 'The Woman With the Handbag'
description: Controversy surrounded the creation of this anti-fascist statue.

name: Hotell Hackspett (Woodpecker Hotel)
description: Unfortunately, you can no longer spend a night in this single-person treetop hotel.

name: Spillings Hoard
description: Buried beneath an unassuming Gotland farm was the largest collection of Viking silver, mostly of Islamic origin.

name: The Unofficial Mosquito-Catching World Championship
description: A Swedish town came up with an out-of-the-box way to eliminate the bloodthirsty insects.

name: The Lagom Standard Measure
description: The standard measure of Lagom, a measure of what is enough.

name: Vanadislunden Water Reservoir
description: A water reservoir disguised as a castle stands atop a hill in a park in Stockholm city.

name: Skissernas Museum
description: The Lund University's former gym houses a museum focusing on the creative process of art.

name: Grimeton VLF Transmitter
description: A Very Low Frequency transmitter you can tune in on your PC.

name: The Landsort Artillery Battery
description: A southern Swedish island is home to the last ERSTA artillery battery, meant to protect the shoreline during the Cold War.

name: Ting1
description: Contrasting the old concrete courthouse beneath, a modern and colorful residential marvel has emerged, stirring both acclaim and controversy.

name: Bullet Button
description: This innocuous-looking button may have been used to assassinate a king.

name: Söderto Fortress
description: This makeshift concrete bunker is an unusual reminder of the Cold War.

name: Meridianen för den Akademiska Kvarten (Meridian for the Academic Quarter)
description: The marker that allows students all over the world to be late for class.

name: Burial Site of Carl Linnaeus
description: The final resting place of the father of modern biological taxonomy.

name: 'Hönstavlan' ('Picturesque Chickens')
description: This strange portrait of six ladies with chicken bodies commemorates a count's dirty habits.

name: Vrak – Museum of Wrecks
description: A museum that dives into Sweden’s maritime past through preserved shipwrecks and interactive exhibits.

name: Bromma Blocks
description: These airport hangars are now a bustling shopping center with only hints of its past still lingering.

name: Wildman
description: The giant club-wielding wildman stands proud as the symbol of Swedish Lapland.

name: Oil Island
description: Few would expect the world's oldest preserved oil refinery to be found in Sweden.

name: AFRY Building
description: From a distance, this structure looks like an ordinary office building but a closer inspection reveals a unique facade.

name: 'Famntaget' ('The Embrace')
description: This statue standing near Sweden's southernmost point was based on the grandmother of a modern movie star.

name: Bureplatsen
description: A massive land art sculpture fills a green space once tarnished by a landfill.

name: The Jerlov Observatory
description: This former peat factory was transformed into an observatory before being abandoned once again.

name: Sverre of Norway Stone
description: Many historians today consider Sverre as Norway's greatest king of all time.

name: Saltsjöbaden Observatory
description: Once the world's most modern observatory, this building is now a private school.

name: Facebook's Arctic Circle Data Center
description: The first overseas server for Facebook in Sweden's tundra.

name: Meridianen för den Akademiska Kvarten (The Real Meridian for the Academic Quarter)
description: A response to the wrongly placed meridian of the same name.

name: Kronblom on a Walk
description: Wooden cutouts of the fictional family from the popular Swedish comic strip take a stroll along the side of the road.

name: MiniKarlsson
description: A miniature statue of a beloved character from children's literature.

name: Zorn Museum
description: The city that saw the birth of Sweden is now home to an impressive collection of work from its favorite artist.

name: The Replica of Fet-Mats
description: A reproduction of a mysteriously preserved body found in the 18th-century and put on display for years before burial.

name: 007 Museum-Nybro
description: Believed to be the first museum dedicated to the films in the world.

name: Where All Powerlines Meet
description: Only spot in the world where an HVDC powerline, a single phase AC and a three phase AC powerline all meet.

name: Skyddsmantelmadonnan
description: A modern, secular take on a traditional Virgin Mary motif.

name: Cirkulation II
description: This simple roadside art installation ended up sparking a folk art movement and a controversy of international proportions.

name: Håverud Aqueduct
description: A railway, a waterway, and a roadway all in one, suspended over a rushing river.

name: Viskafors Blå Får (Blue Sheep of Viskafors)
description: A flock of peculiar-colored sheep graze in this small Swedish town.

name: The Obama Sweden Toilet
description: A Swedish university commemorates the former U.S. President’s bathroom visit with a gold plaque.

name: The Sigtuna Phone Box Library
description: A Swedish phone box converted into an informal village library.

name: Landsorts Lighthouse
description: The oldest Swedish built lighthouse still guides ships through the archipelago.

name: Sweden's Geographical Midpoint
description: The "geometrical" center of the country, as calculated in 1947.

name: The Ghost Plaque of the Keyhole House
description: This mysterious sign claims an American astronomer haunts the building—but is it true?

name: Homografiska Museet
description: This small museum explores questions of representation, diversity, and identity.

name: Whiskey on the Rocks
description: A plaque on this tiny Swedish island commemorates an unusual Cold War standoff of Soviet submarine intrigue.

name: 'The Swedish Melancholy'
description: Look closely, and you'll see this rusty man is crying.

name: Sweden's Oldest Olive Tree
description: A centuries-old olive tree thrives in a small botanical garden.

name: 'A Study in Unhuman Sexual Expectations'
description: These whimsical rabbit figures are not as innocent as they might look.

name: Galgbacken (Gallow Hill)
description: The site of Stockholm's last public execution.

name: Play Shoreline Monument
description: Dedicated to a song so popular the request to "Play Shoreline" became part of Swedish culture.

name: Stenberget (Stone Mountain)
description: According to legend, this structure was inspired by the Tower of Babel and built in a 19th-century industrialist's yard so he could walk his dogs without leaving home.

name: Sigtuna Rådhus (City Hall)
description: A tiny town hall—in Sweden's one-time capital—might be the smallest in Europe.

name: Vittangi Snaps Pine
description: Few remain of these distinctive old trees that once served as natural landmarks for travelers on the road.

name: 'Havsprinsen' ('The Sea Prince')
description: Malmö’s bronze prince yearns for Copenhagen's 'Little Mermaid'—and his missing crown jewels.

name: Gammelstads Kyrkstad (Gammelstad Church Town)
description: A medieval village, with red wooden cottages around a stone church, stands as a well-preserved example of a settlement type that was once common in northern Sweden.

name: The Unmarked Grave of Christer Pettersson
description: Not far from where one of the most famous Swedes of all time is buried, an infamous man was put to rest.

name: Snuvestuan (Cottage of the Hulder)
description: This cave at the base of an unusually shaped cliff is said to be inhabited by a mythical forest enchantres from Scandinavian folklore.

name: Upplevelsdusch
description: This spa shower is an interactive art piece that reflects the feeling of Scandinavian seasons.

name: Onsala Space Observatory
description: An impressive collection of massive radio satellites.

name: The Original Ornäsbjork
description: A semi-sterile mutant birch that became Sweden's national tree.

name: Bergdala Glastekniska Museum
description: The machines behind the famous handblown glass from Sweden's "Kingdom of Crystal."

name: Ljusen i Smedjeån
description: A sculpture of three large candles in the middle of the water of the river Smedjeån.

name: Malmö Spetsbunker (Malmö Winkel Tower)
description: This cone-shaped bomb shelter, modeled on German design, is a rare WWII relic that never saw use but still looms over the harbor.

name: 'Pierre Brassau' Paintings at Borås Zoo
description: The art critic claiming that "only an ape could have done this" was in fact right.

name: Shoemaker's Pear Tree
description: Where a troll might repair your shoes in exchange for a bowl of porridge.

name: Lund Observatory Clock
description: This antique clock once gave the most accurate time in the area.

name: Orrholmsgaraget (The Orrholm Garage)
description: This underground car park moonlights as a permanent art exhibition.

name: Klockhuset I Skadom (The Skadom Clock House)
description: Some clocks inside this museum are more than 500 years old.

name: 'Scanisaurus'
description: These life-sized mosaic reptiles, crafted using ceramics from a local toilet factory, link the town's industrial heritage with its prehistoric past.

name: 'Badaren - Spår av Mänsklighet (The Swimmer - Traces of Man)'
description: An art installation composed of items left behind by beachgoers.

name: Birka Viking Burial Mounds
description: Sweden's first Viking city is also the site of thousands of burial mounds.

name: Börje’s Tie Museum
description: Around 9,000 ties call this museum home.

name: Falks Grav
description: The final resting place of a young stagecoach robber who refused mercy.

name: 'Apan och Kaninen' ('Bunny and Monkey')
description: These massive animal sculptures were constructed as part of a reality TV show challenge.

name: The Devil Head of Raus Church
description: Legend has it that this church faced a troll problem, leading to the affixing of a stone face of Satan above the entrance to deter further encounters.

name: (Alleged) Piece of Descartes' Skull
description: Could this controversial parietal bone be the remains of the famed French philosopher?

name: Oskar Kleins Gastbok
description: The signatures of esteemed colleagues of the physicists who stopped by to attend lectures.

name: Museum of Legends
description: A museum dedicated to the stories and folklore of Småland, Sweden.

name: King Gustav Vasa Knife Incident Plaque
description: This bronze marker acknowledges an embarrassing royal blunder.

name: Trolltrappan
description: A mysterious stone staircase deep in the Swedish woods, said to have been made by trolls.

name: Stenkullen ACDC Pylons
description: Only electricity pylons carrying both AC and DC circuits.

name: Björn Borg's Garage Door
description: The tennis legend became the best in the world (partly) thanks to practicing against his garage door, now on display in a museum near his childhood home.

name: Deutsche Strasse Memorial Stone
description: This lonely rock along a gravel road outside of the southern Swedish village of Eksjö reminds us of a dark chapter in Swedish post-war history.

name: Kungens Kurva Shoppingcenter (King's Bend Mall)
description: An entire shopping mall and industrial terrain named for the time the king of Sweden's car went into a ditch.

name: Drakmärket (Dragon Mark)
description: According to local lore, a flying dragon was blinded by a lighthouse and crashed, leaving a permanent mark on this island rock.

name: Birch Alley
description: A collection of birches from across Sweden.

name: Luffar-Lasse Roundabout
description: A mosaic tribute to a hitchhiking cat turned national celebrity.

name: 'Listen'
description: This disputed feline sculpture is Sweden's first MeToo monument.

name: Ekebol’s Cave
description: Location of a royal cave rave in the 1800s, now largely forgotten.

name: Site of Doverstorp Refugee Camp
description: A few crumbling buildings and the occasional pipe sticking out of the ground are all that remains of the largest refugee camp in Sweden during World War II.

name: Self-Tribute Stone of 'Bagga-Sven'
description: A confident and eccentric goat farmer boldly proclaimed his honor with an inscription on this woodland stone.

name: Gunnar’s Carousel
description: Deep in the Swedish forest hides a forgotten water-powered carousel.

name: World's Largest Skis
description: This Swedish village shows off its most well-known products in a big way.

name: Årjängstrollet
description: This giant wooden sculpture is based on local folklore and is said to be "possibly" Sweden's largest troll.

name: Pilt Carin Ersdotter Statue
description: A sculpture of a woman so beautiful she got arrested for it.

name: Tomtehuset (The Gnome House)
description: A 19th-century home, embellished with gnomes.

name: Haunted Museum of Borås
description: Sweden’s first haunted museum showcases cursed dolls, mirrors, and relics said to carry lingering spirits.

name: Bosse Östlin Statue
description: A heated sculpture pays tribute to an actor who loved hugs.

name: Potato Roundabout
description: This traffic circle in a small Swedish town is adorned with a statue of a giant potato.

name: 'Multiplera Skåne'
description: Unexpectedly standing in a glade, this large group of life-sized, 3D-printed clay figures captures the essence of local young people.

name: Mountaineers Hall of Fame
description: An unassuming plaque with a laminated poster shows the names of people who completed one of the most grueling journeys in Sweden.

name: Sala Silver Mine
description: Sweden’s legendary mine with wildly deep tunnels.

name: 'Gzim och den frusna sjön' ('Gzim and the Frozen Lake')
description: A sculpture capturing the emotional moment when a refugee boy realizes he is about to be deported.

name: 'Fideicommissum'
description: A bronze, peeing self-portrait protests unequal inheritance rights.

name: ‘Way to Go’
description: A bronze sculpture on a bridge, of the shoes of 29 famous Malmöites.

name: Svärjarehålorna (The Swearer's Holes)
description: Is the centuries-old enigma of these mysterious "perjurer's footprints," where nothing ever grows, attributed to natural phenomena or to the realm of folklore?

name: Match Museum
description: One of the world’s only museums dedicated to the humble matchstick.

name: Björketorp Runestone
description: In the Swedish forest stands a stone with a deadly inscription, promising ruin and death to whoever disturbs it.

name: Kvilleken (Rumskulla Oak)
description: This oak has seen Vikings, the Black Death, and world wars pass but refuses to give up.

name: Kalkugnarna
description: Preserved 19th-century lime kilns sit in southern Sweden.

name: Måndals Fort
description: A century-old fort from Stockholm’s “Sausage Line” defenses.

name: The Devil on Linnégatan
description: A devilish mask still mocks a king’s rumored love nest across the street.

name: Gann’s Deserted Church
description: Old ruins of a church from the 1200s.

name: Flight Monument at Anten
description: An American crash site from WWII is marked deep in a Swedish forest.

name: Smöjen Kalkbrottsbad
description: An abandoned industrial site turned into a picturesque swimming hole.

name: Berlin Wall Section in Trelleborg
description: A piece of Cold War history most people walk past unknowingly.

name: Eriksbergskranen
description: Sweden's largest remaining gantry crane is a relic of Gothenburg's industrial past.

name: Göteborgs Remfabrik - Textile Heritage Centre
description: The best-preserved weaving mill from the early 20th century in all of Scandinavia

name: Karin Boye Statue
description: Visitors sometimes leave flowers in honor of this writer and lesbian icon.

name: Kungsgrottan (King's Cave)
description: An Ice Age rock grotto autographed by royalty.

name: Hagavågen (Haga Wave)
description: Artwork created by artists and school children as a tribute to free speech and democracy.

name: Rubber Tyre Duck
description: A rubber duck made out of a decidedly different kind of rubber.

name: Hultanäs Station
description: A little piece of railroad history preserved on a narrow-gauge railway

name: Gustaf Dalén AGA Lighthouse
description: This beacon honors a Nobel Prize-winner whose technology powered its light.

name: Pellerin’s Margarine Factory
description: This factory dominated the Swedish market, and is now a historic site.

name: Storkyrkobadet
description: A hidden bathhouse in one of Stockholm's oldest and busiest neighborhoods is open to anyone who can find it.

name: Museum of Failure
description: From Coke II to Google Glass, this exhibit features some of the most spectacular commercial flops.

name: Stockholms Spelmuseum
description: It's always game time at Stockholm's only museum devoted solely to video games.

name: Tiny Mouse Shops of Malmö
description: Swedish mice can dine at the Nuts of Life restaurant or take a date to the amousement park.

name: Biologiska Museet
description: Early habitat dioramas and home of the elusive gaff, the skvader.

name: Sala Silver Mine Hotel Suite
description: This luxury hotel suite is located deep in the bowels of an old mine.

name: Talking Lamp
description: 20-foot tall lamp mumbles Swedish to celebrate the holidays.

name: Björnhammaren
description: Once the nail factory shut its doors, this Swedish factory town was abandoned, leaving behind a modern ghost town.

name: Sjö-Gunnarsbo Sanatorium
description: An abandoned, deteriorating tuberculosis hospital from the turn of the 20th century.

name: The Tuben or "Elk Showers"
description: Tiny Swedish village with a distinct local phenomenon known as an "Elk Shower."

name: Brännaberget Folkpark
description: This abandoned Swedish folkpark offers an amazing view over the town of Överkalix.

name: Slottsskogen Pacifier Tree
description: There are no crying babies in this neck of the woods.

name: Entreprenörskyrkan
description: When a conglomerate of visionary startups commandeer a disused Greek Orthodox Church, zaniness ensues.

name: Emoji Stained Glass Windows
description: These unique stained glass windows were designed by local children using a modern flair.

name: Kiruna Cage Balconies
description: These coffin-like Brutalist balconies were inspired by a nearby mine.

name: Kungens Rondell (King's Carousel)
description: This roundabout was renamed in honor of the Swedish king's royal fender-bender.

name: Flygbussarna
description: Sculpture of a bus made from wrecked passenger cars.

name: Soda Nation
description: Scandinavia’s largest shop dedicated to anything carbonated.

name: Nobel Ice Cream
description: This legendary dessert was served to Nobel laureates for decades.
